perturbation.explanation: By removing the last sentence of Paragraph 4, a key clarification is lost: 'For the purposes of this Paragraph 4, information shall not be deemed to be in the public domain merely because any part of said information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations thereof are now, or become, known to the public, provided, however, that the obligations of Paragraph 3 hereof shall not apply to any such part of said information.' Without this sentence, it becomes easier for the Receiving Party to argue that information is in the public domain, weakening the confidentiality protections outlined in Paragraph 3. This omission contradicts the spirit of maintaining confidentiality and makes Paragraph 3 harder to enforce.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the final sentence in Paragraph 4 significantly weakens the confidentiality protections afforded to MyPoints.com's information, as explained in the 'Perturbation Explanation'. The original text clearly defined the scope of 'public domain' exclusion, preventing the Receiving Party from easily claiming information was in the public domain. Without this sentence, the Receiving Party can argue individual features are now public knowledge, thereby weakening the restrictions set forth in Paragraph 3 and directly contradicting the obligation in the Contradicted Text which prevents the Receiving Party from disclosing or disseminating the information without MyPoints.com's permission. The change creates a clear conflict, making enforcement of the confidentiality clause harder and creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information shall terminate five (5) years from the date of the receipt thereof by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party's obligations with respect to each item of Confidential Information shall terminate five (5) years from the date of the receipt thereof by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing', the agreement becomes inflexible. If both parties, after some years have passed, determine a need for perpetual protection, they will be held back by a strict termination after 5 years, which can be seen as a contradiction of the intention for both parties. The enforceability of this is more rigid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties and supersedes all prior communications, agreements and understandings relating to the subject matter hereof. The provisions of this Agreement may not be modified, amended nor waived, except by a written instrument duly executed by both parties. This Agreement may not be assigned by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of the MyPoints.com. This Agreement is made subject to, and shall be construed under, the laws of the State of Illinois.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for modification of the termination date with mutual written agreement. The changed text removes this flexibility, creating a rigid termination after 5 years. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly states that the agreement may only be modified by a written instrument duly executed by both parties. The change makes it impossible to alter the termination date, contradicting the mechanism for amendments defined in the contradicted text. Thus, a strong in-text contradiction is introduced.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Receiving Party becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide MyPoints.com with prompt notice so that it may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event MyPoints.com is unable to obtain such protective order or other appropriate remedy, only that portion of the Confidential Information which has been deemed by a written opinion of counsel to be legally required to be furnished, shall be disclosed, and Receiving Party will cooperate with the MyPoints.com to obtain a protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Confidential Information so disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Receiving Party becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which has been deemed by a written opinion of counsel to be legally required to be furnished.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section where the Receiving Party should provide MyPoints.com with prompt notice, it eliminates MyPoints.com's opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance, creating a contradiction. This absence of prior notice directly undermines MyPoints.com's rights within the agreement and their ability to protect their confidential information, potentially leading to a violation if information outside the scope of legal obligation is inadvertently revealed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agree that monetary damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and that MyPoints.com shall be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief as a remedy for any such breach of this Agreement, but said remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. It is further agreed that this Agreement is made for the benefit of MyPoints.com, and that no failure or delay by MyPoints.com to enforce its rights hereunder shall operate as a waiver of any right, power or privilege under this Agreement, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided MyPoints.com the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance if the Receiving Party was legally compelled to disclose confidential information. The changed text removes this opportunity. The contradicted text states that MyPoints.com is entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement, including the disclosure of confidential information. By removing the notice provision, MyPoints.com is deprived of the ability to prevent such a breach, directly contradicting their entitlement to remedies for a breach of the agreement. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition because the removal of the notice provision creates a conflict with MyPoints.com's rights to remedies.
